English Sentence: I felt so relieved when I heard that the operation had gone well.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 當我聽說手術進展順利時，我感到非常鬆了一口氣。
Grammar / Usage Note: felt relieved + when clause

English Sentence: She was overwhelmed with emotion when she crossed the finish line.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 當佢衝過終點線嗰陣，佢非常激動 / 百感交集。
Grammar / Usage Note: overwhelmed with emotion + when clause

English Sentence: He felt guilty about missing his friend's birthday party even though he had a good reason.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 即使他有充分的理由，他仍然對錯過朋友的生日派對感到愧疚。
Grammar / Usage Note: felt guilty + even though clause

English Sentence: I was devastated when I found out I hadn't got the job I had been preparing for.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 當我知我拎唔到份工嗰陣，我好難受 / 崩潰。
Grammar / Usage Note: was devastated + when + past perfect

English Sentence: She was so excited about the trip that she couldn't sleep the night before.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她對旅行太興奮了，以至於前一晚無法入睡。
Grammar / Usage Note: so excited that + consequence

English Sentence: He gets anxious in crowded places and prefers smaller, quieter environments.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他在擁擠的地方會感到焦慮，更喜歡更小、更安靜的環境。
Grammar / Usage Note: gets anxious in + prefers for personality description

English Sentence: I sometimes feel lonely even when I am surrounded by people.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 即使周圍有很多人，我有時也會感到孤獨。
Grammar / Usage Note: even when clause for paradox

English Sentence: She felt incredibly proud of herself after completing the marathon.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 完成馬拉松後，她為自己感到非常驕傲。
Grammar / Usage Note: felt proud of herself + after + gerund

English Sentence: He was deeply moved by the kindness of strangers during a difficult time.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 在困難時期，陌生人的善意深深地感動了他。
Grammar / Usage Note: was deeply moved by + noun phrase

English Sentence: I feel most content when I am spending quality time with the people I love.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 當我和我愛的人共度美好時光時，我感到最滿足。
Grammar / Usage Note: feel most + adjective + when clause

English Sentence: She struggled with self-doubt for years before she finally believed in her own abilities.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她掙扎於自我懷疑多年，才最終相信自己的能力。
Grammar / Usage Note: struggled with + before finally

English Sentence: He admitted that he had been bottling up his feelings and needed to talk to someone.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他承認他一直在壓抑自己的情緒，需要找人傾訴。
Grammar / Usage Note: admitted that + past perfect continuous

English Sentence: I find it helpful to write down my thoughts when I am feeling stressed or overwhelmed.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 當我感到壓力或不知所措時，我發現把想法寫下來很有幫助。
Grammar / Usage Note: I find it helpful + to + infinitive + when clause

English Sentence: She experienced a wave of nostalgia when she heard the song that had played at her graduation.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 當她聽到她畢業典禮上播放的那首歌時，她感到了一陣懷舊之情。
Grammar / Usage Note: experienced a wave of + when clause

English Sentence: He tries not to let his emotions affect his professional decisions, but it isn't always easy.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他盡量不讓情緒影響他的職業決策，但這並不總是容易的。
Grammar / Usage Note: tries not to + but clause for honesty

English Sentence: She has been best friends with her neighbour since they were both five years old.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她和鄰居從五歲開始就是最好的朋友了。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect with since for lifelong friendship

English Sentence: He and his partner met at university and have been together for over twelve years.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他和他的伴侶在大學相識，已經在一起超過十二年了。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple + present perfect continuous

English Sentence: I find that strong relationships are built on trust, communication, and mutual respect.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我發現牢固的關係是建立在信任、溝通和相互尊重上的。
Grammar / Usage Note: I find that + are built on + list

English Sentence: She apologised for her behaviour and made a genuine effort to repair the friendship.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她為自己的行為道歉，並真誠地努力修復友誼。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple + made a genuine effort to

English Sentence: He keeps in contact with his old school friends despite living in different countries.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 儘管住在不同的國家，他仍與老同學保持聯繫。
Grammar / Usage Note: despite + gerund for contrast

English Sentence: I think it is important to make time for the people who matter most in your life.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我認為為生命中最重要的人騰出時間是很重要的。
Grammar / Usage Note: I think it is important to + infinitive

English Sentence: She supported him unconditionally through a very difficult period in his life.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她在他生命中非常困難的時期無條件地支持他。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple with unconditionally for loyalty

English Sentence: He finds it difficult to open up to people he doesn't know very well.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他發現很難向他不太熟悉的人敞開心扉。
Grammar / Usage Note: finds it difficult to + infinitive + who clause

English Sentence: I realised how much my family meant to me when I moved abroad for the first time.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 當我第一次出國時，我意識到家人對我來說有多重要。
Grammar / Usage Note: realised how much + when clause

English Sentence: She grew up in a very close-knit family where everyone looked out for each other.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她在一個非常緊密的家庭中長大，每個人都互相照顧。
Grammar / Usage Note: grew up in + where clause

English Sentence: He has been a mentor to many junior colleagues over the course of his career.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 在他的職業生涯中，他是許多初級同事的導師。
Grammar / Usage Note: present perfect for career-long role

English Sentence: I try to be a good listener rather than always offering advice when friends come to me with problems.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 當朋友帶著問題找我時，我盡量做一個好的傾聽者，而不是總是提供建議。
Grammar / Usage Note: try to be + rather than + when clause

English Sentence: She forgave him for the mistake, which showed just how strong their friendship was.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她原諒了他的錯誤，這表明了他們的友誼有多深厚。
Grammar / Usage Note: past simple + which showed how

English Sentence: He was the first person I called when I received the good news.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他是我收到好消息時第一個打電話的人。
Grammar / Usage Note: first person + when clause for closeness

English Sentence: I have learned that relationships require constant effort and cannot be taken for granted.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我了解到關係需要持續的努力，不能視為理所當然。
Grammar / Usage Note: have learned that + cannot be taken for granted

English Sentence: Living in the city is much more convenient than living in the countryside, but it can also be much more stressful.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 住在城市比住在農村方便得多，但也可能壓力大得多。
Grammar / Usage Note: comparative adjectives with but for contrast

English Sentence: She is far more organised than her brother, who tends to leave everything to the last minute.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她比她的哥哥有條理得多，她的哥哥往往把所有事情都留到最後一刻。
Grammar / Usage Note: far more + who clause for contrast

English Sentence: This year's results are significantly better than last year's, which is a reflection of the team's hard work.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 今年的結果比去年好得多，這反映了團隊的努力。
Grammar / Usage Note: significantly better than + which clause

English Sentence: He is not nearly as experienced as his colleague, but he makes up for it with his enthusiasm.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他遠沒有他的同事有經驗，但他用熱情彌補了這一點。
Grammar / Usage Note: not nearly as... as + but makes up for it

English Sentence: The new version of the app is twice as fast and uses half the battery life of the old one.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 新版本的應用程式速度是舊版本的兩倍，電池消耗只有舊版的一半。
Grammar / Usage Note: twice as + adjective + half the noun

English Sentence: Renting is generally cheaper in the short term, whereas buying is more cost-effective in the long run.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 短期來看租房通常更便宜，而長期來看買房更具成本效益。
Grammar / Usage Note: whereas for formal contrast with time expressions

English Sentence: She speaks French far more fluently than she did when she first moved to Paris.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她說法語比她剛搬到巴黎時流利得多。
Grammar / Usage Note: comparative + than + when clause for progress

English Sentence: The older model was simpler but more reliable; the newer one has more features but breaks down more often.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 舊型號更簡單但更可靠；新型號功能更多但更容易出故障。
Grammar / Usage Note: semi-colon for parallel comparison

English Sentence: He prefers reading physical books to reading on a screen, even though e-books are more portable.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 即使電子書更便攜，他仍然更喜歡讀實體書而不是在螢幕上閱讀。
Grammar / Usage Note: prefer + gerund to + gerund + even though

English Sentence: The second interview was much harder than the first, which caught her off guard.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 第二次面試比第一次難多了，這讓她措手不及。
Grammar / Usage Note: much harder than + which caught off guard

English Sentence: Working collaboratively is often more effective than working alone, provided everyone contributes equally.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 只要每個人都平等地做出貢獻，協作工作往往比單獨工作更有效。
Grammar / Usage Note: comparative + provided clause for condition

English Sentence: She earns considerably more than she did five years ago, yet she lives just as simply.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她的收入比五年前多得多，但她的生活依然同樣簡樸。
Grammar / Usage Note: considerably more + yet + just as for contrast

English Sentence: The countryside is far quieter than the city, but it lacks the entertainment options that city life offers.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 農村比城市安靜得多，但缺乏城市生活所提供的娛樂選擇。
Grammar / Usage Note: far quieter + but lacks for trade-off

English Sentence: This café is not as well-known as the one on the main street, but the coffee is just as good.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這家咖啡廳不像大街上那家那麼有名，但咖啡品質同樣好。
Grammar / Usage Note: not as + as + but just as

English Sentence: He runs much faster than average for his age, which is a testament to years of dedicated training.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他的跑步速度比同齡人平均快得多，這證明了多年的專注訓練。
Grammar / Usage Note: much faster than + which is a testament to

English Sentence: She missed the deadline because she had underestimated how long the project would take.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她錯過了截止日期，因為她低估了這個專案需要多長時間。
Grammar / Usage Note: because + past perfect for reason

English Sentence: He failed the exam as a result of not studying enough in the weeks leading up to it.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他考試不及格，原因是在考試前幾週學習不夠努力。
Grammar / Usage Note: as a result of + gerund phrase

English Sentence: The company lost several clients due to poor communication and slow response times.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 由於溝通不暢和回應時間慢，公司失去了幾個客戶。
Grammar / Usage Note: due to + noun list for cause

English Sentence: She was so tired after the long journey that she fell asleep as soon as she got home.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她長途旅行後太累了，一回到家就睡著了。
Grammar / Usage Note: so tired that + as soon as clause

English Sentence: He invested heavily in his education, which led to better job opportunities later in life.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他在教育上投入了大量資金，這在他的後半生帶來了更好的工作機會。
Grammar / Usage Note: which led to for chain effect

English Sentence: The heavy rain caused flooding throughout the city, resulting in major disruptions to transport.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 大暴雨導致整個城市發生洪水，造成交通嚴重中斷。
Grammar / Usage Note: caused + present participle for cascading effect

English Sentence: I started feeling much better once I changed my diet and began exercising regularly.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 一旦我改變了飲食並開始定期運動，我就開始感覺好多了。
Grammar / Usage Note: once clause for trigger of improvement

English Sentence: She earned a promotion owing to her consistently excellent performance over the past year.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 由於她在過去一年中始終如一的出色表現，她獲得了晉升。
Grammar / Usage Note: owing to + noun phrase for formal reason

English Sentence: The project succeeded largely because the team communicated openly and worked well together.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這個專案的成功主要是因為團隊坦誠溝通並相互合作。
Grammar / Usage Note: largely because + clause for primary reason

English Sentence: He developed a strong interest in environmental issues as a consequence of reading a powerful book.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他因閱讀了一本有力的書而對環境問題產生了濃厚的興趣。
Grammar / Usage Note: as a consequence of + gerund

English Sentence: Their relationship improved dramatically once they started having honest conversations.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 一旦他們開始進行坦誠的對話，他們的關係就顯著改善了。
Grammar / Usage Note: once clause for trigger with dramatic result

English Sentence: The lack of sleep led to poor concentration, which affected her overall productivity.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 睡眠不足導致注意力不集中，影響了她的整體生產力。
Grammar / Usage Note: led to + which affected for chain

English Sentence: He was unable to attend the conference due to a last-minute change in his schedule.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 由於日程安排在最後一刻發生變化，他無法參加會議。
Grammar / Usage Note: due to + noun phrase for formal excuse

English Sentence: Since she started meditating daily, she has noticed a significant reduction in her anxiety levels.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 自從她開始每天冥想以來，她注意到自己的焦慮水準顯著降低。
Grammar / Usage Note: since clause + present perfect for change

English Sentence: The new policy had unintended consequences that made the situation more complicated than before.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 新政策產生了意想不到的後果，使情況比以前更加複雜。
Grammar / Usage Note: had unintended consequences + that clause

English Sentence: If I have enough time this weekend, I will visit my grandparents.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果我這個週末有足夠的時間，我會去探望祖父母。
Grammar / Usage Note: first conditional for likely future

English Sentence: If she studies consistently, she will pass her exams without any problems.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果她持續用功學習，她會毫無問題地通過考試。
Grammar / Usage Note: first conditional for expected outcome

English Sentence: If he gets the promotion, he is going to take his family on a holiday to celebrate.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果他獲得晉升，他打算帶家人去度假慶祝。
Grammar / Usage Note: first conditional with going to

English Sentence: If you don't leave now, you will miss the last train.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果你現在不出發，你會錯過最後一班火車。
Grammar / Usage Note: first conditional negative for warning

English Sentence: If I were you, I would think carefully before making such an important decision.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果我是你，我會在做這麼重要的決定之前仔細考慮。
Grammar / Usage Note: second conditional with If I were you for advice

English Sentence: She would travel more often if she had more annual leave from work.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果她有更多年假，她會更經常旅行。
Grammar / Usage Note: second conditional for hypothetical wish

English Sentence: He would apply for the job if he felt more confident about his qualifications.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果他對自己的資格更有信心，他會申請這份工作。
Grammar / Usage Note: second conditional for confidence barrier

English Sentence: If I won the lottery, I would pay off my mortgage and donate the rest to charity.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果我中了樂透，我會還清房貸並把剩下的捐給慈善機構。
Grammar / Usage Note: second conditional for hypothetical windfall

English Sentence: She would be so much happier if she spent less time worrying about things she cannot control.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果她少花時間擔心她無法控制的事情，她會快樂得多。
Grammar / Usage Note: second conditional with comparative result

English Sentence: If I had known about the traffic, I would have taken a different route.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果我事先知道交通狀況，我就會走另一條路線了。
Grammar / Usage Note: third conditional for past regret

English Sentence: She would have got the job if she had been better prepared for the interview.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果她在面試前準備得更充分，她就會得到那份工作了。
Grammar / Usage Note: third conditional for past missed opportunity

English Sentence: He would have been on time if his alarm had gone off as planned.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果他的鬧鐘按計劃響了，他就能準時到了。
Grammar / Usage Note: third conditional for past blame

English Sentence: If they had communicated better, the misunderstanding could have been avoided.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果他們溝通得更好，這次誤解本可以避免的。
Grammar / Usage Note: third conditional with could have been

English Sentence: Provided that the weather is good, we will hold the event outdoors as planned.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 只要天氣好，我們就按計劃在戶外舉辦活動。
Grammar / Usage Note: provided that for formal condition

English Sentence: As long as you keep practising every day, your English will improve steadily.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 只要你每天持續練習，你的英文就會穩步提高。
Grammar / Usage Note: as long as for ongoing condition with result

English Sentence: Unless she changes her approach, she is unlikely to see any improvement.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 除非她改變方式，否則她不太可能看到任何改進。
Grammar / Usage Note: unless for negative condition

English Sentence: Even if it rains, the outdoor event will go ahead as scheduled.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 即使下雨，戶外活動也將按計劃進行。
Grammar / Usage Note: even if for determined outcome regardless

English Sentence: Suppose you were offered a job abroad, would you take it?
Traditional Chinese Translation: 假設你被提供了一份海外工作，你會接受嗎？
Grammar / Usage Note: suppose + past simple for hypothetical question

English Sentence: Whether or not you agree with the decision, you are expected to follow the policy.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 無論你是否同意這個決定，你都應該遵守政策。
Grammar / Usage Note: whether or not for inclusive condition

English Sentence: Should you need any further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact us.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果您需要進一步的協助，請隨時與我們聯繫。
Grammar / Usage Note: inverted conditional should + infinitive for formal

English Sentence: I am writing to enquire about the availability of the position advertised on your website.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我寫信是為了詢問貴公司網站上刊登職位的空缺情況。
Grammar / Usage Note: formal letter opening for enquiry

English Sentence: Please find attached the documents you requested at our meeting last Thursday.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 請查收我上週四會議中您所要求的文件附件。
Grammar / Usage Note: please find attached for email attachment

English Sentence: I would be grateful if you could confirm receipt of this email at your earliest convenience.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果您能在方便的時候確認收到此電子郵件，我將不勝感激。
Grammar / Usage Note: I would be grateful if + could for polite request

English Sentence: Further to our conversation yesterday, I wanted to follow up on the points we discussed.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 繼昨天我們的對話之後，我想跟進我們討論的幾點。
Grammar / Usage Note: Further to + previous interaction for email follow-up

English Sentence: We regret to inform you that your application has not been successful on this occasion.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們遺憾地通知您，您這次的申請未能成功。
Grammar / Usage Note: We regret to inform for formal rejection

English Sentence: I would like to take this opportunity to express my sincere gratitude for your support.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我想藉此機會表達我對您支持的誠摯感謝。
Grammar / Usage Note: I would like to take this opportunity to for formal thanks

English Sentence: Should you have any questions or concerns, please do not hesitate to get in touch.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果您有任何問題或疑慮，請隨時與我們聯繫。
Grammar / Usage Note: should + not hesitate to for formal closing

English Sentence: The purpose of this report is to outline the key findings of our recent survey.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 本報告的目的是概述我們最近調查的主要發現。
Grammar / Usage Note: The purpose of this + to + infinitive for formal intro

English Sentence: I am pleased to confirm that the arrangements have been finalised as discussed.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我很高興確認安排已按討論內容確定。
Grammar / Usage Note: I am pleased to confirm for formal positive update

English Sentence: We would appreciate it if you could provide us with your feedback by the end of the week.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果您能在本週結束前向我們提供反饋，我們將不勝感激。
Grammar / Usage Note: We would appreciate it if + could for formal request

English Sentence: In light of the recent developments, we have decided to postpone the launch until further notice.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 鑑於近期的發展，我們決定將發布推遲到另行通知。
Grammar / Usage Note: In light of + we have decided to for formal announcement

English Sentence: I look forward to hearing from you and hope we can arrange a meeting at your convenience.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我期待您的回覆，並希望能在您方便的時候安排一次會議。
Grammar / Usage Note: I look forward to + gerund for formal closing

English Sentence: The report highlights several areas for improvement and recommends a number of key actions.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 報告強調了幾個需要改進的領域，並建議了一些關鍵行動。
Grammar / Usage Note: highlights + and recommends for formal report language

English Sentence: We hereby confirm the details of your order as outlined in the attached invoice.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們在此確認您的訂單詳情，如附件發票所示。
Grammar / Usage Note: hereby + as outlined in for legal/formal confirmation

English Sentence: It has come to our attention that there may be an error in the information previously provided.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我們注意到之前提供的資訊可能有誤。
Grammar / Usage Note: It has come to our attention that for formal problem statement

English Sentence: You should see a doctor if the pain doesn't improve within a few days.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果幾天後疼痛沒有改善，你應該去看醫生。
Grammar / Usage Note: should for advice + if clause

English Sentence: She must have worked very hard to achieve such impressive results.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她一定非常努力地工作才能取得如此令人印象深刻的成果。
Grammar / Usage Note: must have + past participle for past deduction

English Sentence: He might be late because his meeting has been running longer than expected.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他可能會遲到，因為他的會議比預期的時間長。
Grammar / Usage Note: might for possibility with reason clause

English Sentence: You must not share confidential information with anyone outside of the company.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 你絕對不能與公司以外的任何人分享機密資訊。
Grammar / Usage Note: must not for prohibition

English Sentence: She could have taken a different approach, but she chose to stick with the original plan.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她本可以採取不同的方法，但她選擇堅持原來的計劃。
Grammar / Usage Note: could have + but chose to for past alternative

English Sentence: He can speak four languages fluently, which is a huge advantage in his line of work.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他能流利地說四種語言，這在他的工作領域是一個巨大的優勢。
Grammar / Usage Note: can for ability + which clause

English Sentence: I should have replied to that email sooner; I hope it didn't cause any inconvenience.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 我應該早點回覆那封電子郵件；我希望這沒有造成任何不便。
Grammar / Usage Note: should have for regret + I hope clause

English Sentence: We may need to revise the timeline if the initial estimates turn out to be inaccurate.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果最初的估計不準確，我們可能需要修改時間表。
Grammar / Usage Note: may need to + if clause for contingency

English Sentence: She had to reschedule her appointment three times before she could finally see the specialist.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 她不得不三次重新安排預約，才能最終見到專科醫生。
Grammar / Usage Note: had to + before finally for frustration and persistence

English Sentence: You don't have to attend every meeting if you feel your time is better spent elsewhere.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 如果你覺得你的時間用在其他地方更有價值，你不必參加每次會議。
Grammar / Usage Note: don't have to for absence of obligation

English Sentence: He would rather wait for the right opportunity than take a job that doesn't suit him.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他寧願等待合適的機會，也不願接受一份不適合他的工作。
Grammar / Usage Note: would rather than for preference

English Sentence: She must be feeling exhausted after working twelve-hour days for the past two weeks.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 在過去兩週每天工作十二小時後，她一定感到筋疲力盡了。
Grammar / Usage Note: must be + gerund for present deduction

English Sentence: I could not have completed the project without the support of my colleagues.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 沒有同事的支持，我不可能完成這個專案。
Grammar / Usage Note: could not have + without phrase for gratitude

English Sentence: They ought to inform their customers before making any significant changes to the service.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 他們應該在對服務進行任何重大更改之前通知客戶。
Grammar / Usage Note: ought to for moral obligation

English Sentence: You might want to double-check the figures before presenting them to the board.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 在向董事會展示數字之前，你可能應該再次確認。
Grammar / Usage Note: might want to for gentle suggestion

English Sentence: This study aims to examine the relationship between sleep quality and academic performance.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 本研究旨在探討睡眠質量與學業表現之間的關係。
Grammar / Usage Note: This study aims to for academic purpose statement

English Sentence: The data suggests that there is a strong correlation between exercise and mental wellbeing.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 數據表明，運動與心理健康之間存在強烈的相關性。
Grammar / Usage Note: The data suggests that for analytical claim

English Sentence: While some researchers argue that technology improves productivity, others contend that it creates more distractions.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 雖然一些研究人員認為技術提高了生產力，但另一些人則主張它造成了更多的干擾。
Grammar / Usage Note: While... others for presenting opposing views

English Sentence: It can be argued that the benefits of remote work outweigh the drawbacks for most employees.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 可以說，對於大多數員工來說，遠距工作的好處大於缺點。
Grammar / Usage Note: It can be argued that for hedged claim

English Sentence: The evidence clearly indicates that early intervention leads to significantly better outcomes.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 證據清楚地表明，早期干預可以帶來顯著更好的結果。
Grammar / Usage Note: The evidence indicates that for strong analytical statement

English Sentence: This essay will explore the key factors that contribute to the success of small businesses.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 本文將探討有助於小型企業成功的關鍵因素。
Grammar / Usage Note: This essay will explore for academic essay intro

English Sentence: In contrast to previous studies, this research adopts a qualitative approach to the topic.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 與以前的研究不同，本研究對這一主題採用了質性研究方法。
Grammar / Usage Note: In contrast to for academic comparison

English Sentence: The findings of this research have significant implications for both policy and practice.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 本研究的發現對政策和實踐都有重要意義。
Grammar / Usage Note: have implications for academic impact statement

English Sentence: To a large extent, the outcome was determined by factors outside of the participants' control.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 在很大程度上，結果是由參與者無法控制的因素決定的。
Grammar / Usage Note: To a large extent for hedged attribution

English Sentence: It is widely acknowledged that climate change poses one of the greatest threats to global stability.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 人們普遍認為，氣候變遷是對全球穩定的最大威脅之一。
Grammar / Usage Note: It is widely acknowledged that for established consensus

English Sentence: Further research is needed in order to draw more definitive conclusions on this matter.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 需要進一步研究才能對這一問題得出更明確的結論。
Grammar / Usage Note: Further research is needed for academic call to action

English Sentence: The results were consistent with the initial hypothesis, thereby supporting the theoretical framework.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 結果與最初的假設一致，從而支持了理論框架。
Grammar / Usage Note: consistent with + thereby supporting for analytical conclusion

English Sentence: Despite the limitations of the study, the conclusions remain relevant and worthy of consideration.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 儘管研究存在局限性，但結論仍然具有相關性和值得考慮的意義。
Grammar / Usage Note: Despite the limitations for balanced academic writing

English Sentence: One possible explanation for this phenomenon is that participants were influenced by social expectations.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這一現象的一種可能解釋是參與者受到社會期望的影響。
Grammar / Usage Note: One possible explanation for hedged interpretation

English Sentence: This analysis reveals that the most significant variable was not the one originally anticipated.
Traditional Chinese Translation: 這項分析揭示了最重要的變數並不是最初預期的那個。
Grammar / Usage Note: This analysis reveals that for strong finding statement
